title: Artificial Intelligence and Digital Tools for Assisting Low-Carbon Architectural Design: Merging the Use of Machine Learning, Large Language Models, and Building Information Modeling for Life Cycle Assessment Tool Development
inputData: 混合数据（BIM、LCA数据库、设计参数）
contribution: 集成ML（CNN预测碳足迹）、LLM（ChatGPT设计助手）和BIM的工具支持建筑师在设计阶段做出低碳决策
pdfFile: Płoszaj-Mazurek et al. - 2024 - Artificial Intelligence and Digital Tools for Assisting Low-Carbon Architectural Design Merging the.pdf
markdownFile: Płoszaj-Mazurek et al. - 2024 - Artificial Intelligence and Digital Tools for Assisting Low-Carbon Architectural Design Merging the.md
abstract: This paper presents an integrated approach combining machine learning, large language models (LLMs), and building information modeling (BIM) to support low-carbon architectural design through Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) tool development. Three case studies are presented: a CNN-based carbon footprint estimation tool for early design stages, an LLM-powered web carbon calculator providing material optimization suggestions, and a 3D web application merging BIM/IFC models with AI-driven carbon analysis. The study demonstrates that AI-assisted tools can meaningfully reduce building carbon footprints during the design phase.
methodology: The study develops three progressive prototype tools: (1) a CNN trained on a simulated dataset of 3300 multifamily buildings to predict carbon footprints from design parameters and grayscale urban-layout images, (2) a web-based carbon calculator integrated with the ChatGPT gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct API for material substitution suggestions, and (3) a web-based 3D application that parses IFC BIM files for automated quantity takeoff and combines LCA calculation with LLM-driven design optimization recommendations.
results: The CNN-based tool achieved a 25% total carbon footprint reduction (from 15.18 to 11.29 kgCO2eq/m2) in a test building by optimizing shape and construction technology. The LLM-assisted tools successfully suggested lower-carbon material replacements (e.g., straw insulation at 12.31 vs. mineral wool at 33.58 kgCO2eq/m3), though some suggestions were architecturally infeasible, highlighting the need for domain-specific AI models. The BIM-integrated 3D application calculated an embodied carbon footprint of 64,120 kgCO2eq for a test building and provided actionable AI-driven optimization suggestions.
inputDataEn: Mixed data (BIM, LCA database, design parameters)
